concerns.text: RQ claims 'increase crop yield stability by ≥30%' (causal) but Methodology proposes 'Regression models' on observational survey data. Without IV, DiD, or RCT design, selection bias (wealthier farmers adopt CSA) invalidates causal claims.
KPI measures 'Yield improvement' (mean), but RQ asks for 'yield stability' (variance). Data model lacks longitudinal yield records per plot to calculate variance over time.
Plan admits 'Soil Property Data... No verified source available'. Soil is a primary confound for yield. Analysis cannot control for this without the data, biasing the CSA impact estimate.
Sample scale (1000+ responses, 5 regions) is insufficient to generalize to 'Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia' as claimed in RQ. External validity is overstated.
